title: Use The Operator Station Emergency Stop Push-Button In An Imminent Danger Situation
summary: Identify the operator station emergency stop push-button and use it only when there is imminent danger to personnel or risk of damage to equipment.
when_to_use: Use this procedure at the operator station when there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment and the documented emergency stop push-button must be used.
not_for: Do not use for normal tipper cycle starts.
Do not use as a substitute for the four normal operator station tipper push-buttons.
Do not use this runbook for emergency stop reset, restart, or restoration because those steps are not provided in this source section.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Identify the emergency stop push-button at the operator station and press it only in the documented imminent danger condition.

safety_notes: The emergency stop button is only used if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.
This source section identifies the emergency stop location and purpose but does not provide reset or restart steps after use.
access_or_tools_needed: Physical access to the operator station
Ability to identify the emergency stop push-button

access_required: Physical access to the operator station
system_preconditions: Operator is at the operator station and can identify the push-button panel.

visual_references.description: Operator station push-button photo showing the emergency stop push-button and the four normal tipper push-buttons.
Reference view of the operator station location and hardware context.

steps.title: Locate the emergency stop push-button
steps.purpose: Ensure the operator identifies the correct emergency control before acting.
steps.instruction: At the operator station, locate the emergency stop push-button and distinguish it from the four normal tipper push-buttons using Figure 3-3 and Table 3-2.
steps.expected_result: The operator can identify the emergency stop push-button as the documented emergency control at the operator station.
steps.healthy_condition: The emergency stop push-button is correctly identified as item 5 and is distinguished from the four normal tipper push-buttons.
steps.failure_condition: The operator cannot confidently distinguish the emergency stop push-button from the normal tipper push-buttons.

actions.label: Identify emergency stop button
actions.exact_action: Locate the emergency stop push-button on the operator station and distinguish it from the four normal tipper push-buttons.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The operator station push-buttons, especially item 5 identified as the emergency stop push-button relative to the left and right tipper buttons.
The overall operator station location and push-button area for physical orientation.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if the emergency stop push-button cannot be positively identified from the operator station controls.
Escalate if the push-button layout does not match the documented Figure 3-3 and Table 3-2 reference.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Table 3-2 identifies item 5 as the emergency stop push-button.
The operator station has four push-buttons and an emergency stop push-button. The top two buttons control the left tipper; the bottom two buttons control the right tipper.

steps.title: Confirm the emergency condition matches the documented use
steps.purpose: Limit use of the emergency stop to the specific safety condition documented by the source.
steps.instruction: Use the emergency stop push-button only if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.
steps.expected_result: The operator confirms the situation matches the documented emergency-use condition before pressing the button.
steps.healthy_condition: There is imminent danger to personnel or risk of equipment damage, matching the documented purpose of the emergency stop.
steps.failure_condition: The situation is not an imminent danger condition, or the operator is considering using the emergency stop for a non-emergency purpose.

actions.label: Assess emergency condition
actions.exact_action: Use the emergency stop push-button only if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The push-button panel showing the emergency stop in the same control group as the normal tipper buttons.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop if there is no imminent danger to personnel or damage to equipment.
Escalate for follow-up recovery after emergency stop use because this source does not provide restoration steps.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The emergency stop button is only used if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.

steps.title: Press the emergency stop push-button
steps.purpose: Execute the documented emergency action at the operator station when the imminent danger condition exists.
steps.instruction: Press the emergency stop push-button when the documented emergency condition exists.
steps.expected_result: The documented emergency stop control is used in response to the imminent danger condition.
steps.healthy_condition: The operator presses the identified emergency stop push-button only for the documented emergency purpose.
steps.failure_condition: The emergency stop is not pressed during an imminent danger condition, or it is pressed for a non-documented purpose.

actions.label: Press emergency stop
actions.exact_action: Press the emergency stop push-button to stop operation in that emergency condition.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The emergency stop push-button on the operator station push-button panel.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate for follow-up recovery because post-E-stop reset or restart steps are not provided in this source section.
Escalate if additional recovery or restoration actions are needed after the emergency stop is used.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The emergency stop button is only used if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.
Table 3-2 identifies item 5 as the emergency stop push-button.

success_criteria: The operator correctly identifies the emergency stop push-button at the operator station.
The emergency stop push-button is used only when there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.
The documented emergency control is used instead of the normal tipper push-buttons during the emergency condition.
healthy_conditions: The emergency stop push-button is identifiable as item 5 in Figure 3-3 and Table 3-2.
The operator understands that the emergency stop is reserved for imminent danger to personnel or equipment damage.
The operator uses the control only for the documented emergency purpose.
failure_conditions: The emergency stop push-button cannot be identified from the operator station controls.
The emergency stop is used for a non-emergency purpose.
Post-emergency reset or restart is attempted using this runbook even though the source does not provide those steps.
escalation_guidance: Escalate for follow-up recovery because this source only documents when to use the emergency stop and where it is located.
Escalate if reset, restart, or restoration of operation is required after emergency stop use because those steps are not provided in this source section.
Escalate if the physical push-button layout does not match the documented Figure 3-3 and Table 3-2 references.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Emergency stop push-button location and its distinction from the four normal tipper push-buttons.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The emergency stop button is only used if there is imminent danger to personnel or damage to the equipment.
Table 3-2 identifies item 5 as the emergency stop push-button.

missing_details: This source section does not describe the resulting machine state after the emergency stop is pressed.
This source section does not provide emergency stop reset steps.
This source section does not provide restart or restoration steps after emergency stop use.
This source section does not provide an estimated completion time.
This source section does not explicitly state whether production stop or LOTO is required as part of this action.
